title: Dichanthelium polycaulon
taxonomy: Dichanthelium polycaulon is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a type of panic grass, specifically belonging to the genus Dichanthelium. Within the Poaceae family, Dichanthelium polycaulon is further classified as part of the subfamily Chloridoideae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships to other grass species. As a member of the Dichanthelium genus, it is closely related to other species within this group.

intro: Dichanthelium polycaulon, also known as woodland grass or panic grass, is a perennial grass species native to North America, characterized by its hairy stems and spikelets. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Dichanthelium polycaulon include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dichanthelium polycaulon is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments for topical applications.
